consistency.level_school_match: Consistent. Evocation is a typical school for protective/damage spells.
consistency.action_economy_match_power: Consistent. A full action for a sustained buff granting resistance and consistent reaction damage seems balanced for a 4th-level spell.

quality.clarity: High. The description clearly outlines the choice between warm (cold resistance/fire damage) and chill (fire resistance/cold damage), duration, light emission, and trigger conditions.
quality.completeness: Complete. Contains all standard D&D 5e spell elements (level, school, components, time, range, duration, effect, classes).
quality.flavor: Good. The imagery of wreathed flames is evocative and matches the mechanics.

mechanics.core_benefit: Grants resistance to either Fire or Cold damage, chosen upon casting.
mechanics.secondary_benefit: Deals 2d8 damage (matching the chosen resistance type in reverse) to any creature hitting the caster with a melee attack within 5ft.
mechanics.balance_check: Resistance is a powerful defensive boost. 2d8 damage upon being hit is a significant deterrent for melee attackers. At 4th level, this is a solid defensive/punishment spell, comparable to other defensive buffs of this tier. The damage type flip (Warm = Fire resistance, Cold damage retribution) is unusual but clearly defined and mechanically sound.
mechanics.scaling_consideration: The 2d8 damage does not scale with higher spell slots, which is standard for many fixed-effect buffs but could be noted as a potential area for future variation if needed.
